title: Was Kaji misinformed or
wikitext:
[]: Was Kaji misinformed, or...?             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Jan 01, 2011 11:30 am Kaji brought Adam to Gendo. Kaji told Misato that Lilith was Adam. Was he seriously confused, deliberately misinforming Misato, or what?

[]: Re: Was Kaji misinformed, or...?             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Jan 01, 2011 12:05 pm AshPhoenix wrote: Kaji brought Adam to Gendo. Kaji told Misato that Lilith was Adam. Was he seriously confused, deliberately misinforming Misato, or what?

[]: Re: Was Kaji misinformed, or...?             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Jan 01, 2011 3:41 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Jan 01, 2011 4:11 pm @SeeleTextOnly: So, where does the 'Second Angel', whose existence is implied by the designation of Sachiel as the 'Third' from the beginning of the series, fit into all this? There are some indications, such as the Rei/Moon imagery (and connection of the Moon to First Impact in #07), Rei's lines in #14 about her awareness of another 'self', and even the designation of the giant on the cross as the 'Main L.C.L Plant', that tend to point to Lilith being a part of the story all along.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Jan 01, 2011 4:16 pm ...

[]: Re: Was Kaji misinformed, or...?             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Sat Jan 01, 2011 5:38 pm AshPhoenix wrote: Kaji brought Adam to Gendo. Kaji told Misato that Lilith was Adam. Was he seriously confused, deliberately misinforming Misato, or what?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 01, 2011 5:55 pm There was a bit of play on this subject here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1916 Though I'm not sure if there was any consensus.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Jan 01, 2011 6:08 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sat Jan 01, 2011 6:18 pm It seems like too major of an issue for it to be a continuity thing that the creators missed, but with these guys, who knows? Maybe Kaji found out the truth about Lilith from SEELE, and wanted to keep it a secret from everybody else. Is there a possibility that he was deliberately misinforming Misato about the giant in the basement? He seems like he'd be a pretty good liar.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 01, 2011 7:04 pm It seems Kaji came to believe that the embryo he brought was grown into the white giant that is actually Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 02, 2011 1:30 am Maybe he thought the sample was the original Adam and the one on the cross fully grown clone (just like the evas) that's been set aside for some nefarious purpose?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 1:36 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: it's all awfully confusing, especially with the 'Oh wait, you're not Adam, you're Lilith!' thing from Kaworu that tries to deal with it while drawing awkward attention to the issue.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Sun Jan 02, 2011 9:22 am You know, what always bothered me in the show was not that Kaji didn't know that the being inside Terminal Dogma was not Adam, but that Misato didn't know it wasn't Adam. I mean, she's one of the few people alive who's actually seen what Adam's fully grown form looks like, and she can't figure out that apart from being big and white Lilith and Adam look nothing alike? What's up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jan 02, 2011 10:28 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: she's one of the few people alive who's actually seen what Adam's fully grown form looks like,

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Jan 02, 2011 11:26 am LOLWRITING Giant in Terminal Dogma being Lilith was propably last minute decision given how even some drafts in ep 24 refer it as ADAM Anyway, fanwank away

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sun Jan 02, 2011 1:55 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Errr, she was a child at the time and Adam was all glowing and explody. Plus, her seeing Adam doesn't mean that that's what an Adam clone would look like.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Jan 02, 2011 2:05 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Jan 02, 2011 2:59 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: You're neglecting the episode 13 dialogue between Gendo and Fuyu in confidence. [...] "It's getting too close to Adam."

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:00 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: .... no, wait, the Gendo response was "We may have to sacrifice the entire Geofront then" ... It is crystal clear what the two are referring to.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Jan 02, 2011 3:08 pm ...
